title: Mastering Database Design
subtitle: From Fundamentals to Modern Architectures
author: Wordsus Team
description: Master the art of designing robust, scalable, and efficient database systems. This guide takes you from the core fundamentals of the relational model and normalization to advanced topics like distributed architectures, transaction management, NoSQL stores, and modern cloud-native database trends.

chapters.title: Introduction to Data and Database Management Systems (DBMS)
chapters.description: Explore the evolution of data storage, core DBMS architecture, and the various roles involved in database design and management.

chapters.title: The Relational Model
chapters.description: Deep dive into the anatomy of relations, keys, integrity constraints, and the foundations of relational algebra.

chapters.title: Data Modeling and the Entity-Relationship (ER) Model
chapters.description: Learn the conceptual design process, identifying entities and relationships, and creating effective ER diagrams.

chapters.title: Advanced ER Modeling Concepts
chapters.description: Understand supertypes, subtypes, weak entities, and how to model complex time-dependent data constraints.

chapters.title: Translating ER Models to Relational Schemas
chapters.description: Master the transformation of conceptual models into logical relational schemas, handling attributes and relationships.

chapters.title: Functional Dependencies and Normalization Fundamentals
chapters.description: Eliminate data anomalies using normalization techniques from First Normal Form (1NF) through Third Normal Form (3NF).

chapters.title: Advanced Normal Forms and Denormalization
chapters.description: Explore BCNF, 4NF, and 5NF, and learn when to strategically denormalize for performance optimization.

chapters.title: Data Definition Language (DDL) and Schema Creation
chapters.description: Implement database schemas using SQL, defining data types, views, triggers, and managing migrations.

chapters.title: Physical Storage, Indexes, and Access Methods
chapters.description: Understand disk storage, buffer management, and the architecture of B-Trees, Hash indexes, and Bitmap indexes.

chapters.title: Query Processing and Optimization
chapters.description: Learn how SQL engines parse, optimize, and execute queries using cost-based and rule-based techniques.

chapters.title: Transaction Concepts and ACID Properties
chapters.description: Master the principles of Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, and Durability (ACID) for reliable data processing.

chapters.title: Concurrency Control Techniques
chapters.description: Solve concurrency problems using locking protocols, MVCC, and optimistic concurrency control mechanisms.

chapters.title: Database Recovery Protocols
chapters.description: Explore undo/redo logging, checkpointing, and algorithms like ARIES to ensure database durability after failures.

chapters.title: Distributed Databases and Replication
chapters.description: Design distributed systems with horizontal fragmentation, 2PC protocols, and understand CAP/PACELC trade-offs.

chapters.title: Data Warehousing and OLAP
chapters.description: Build analytical systems using star/snowflake schemas, ETL pipelines, and multidimensional data cubes.

chapters.title: NoSQL Databases and Non-Relational Models
chapters.description: Compare key-value, document, column-family, and graph databases for specialized scaling requirements.

chapters.title: Database Security and Authorization
chapters.description: Implement robust security with access control models, encryption, and prevention of SQL injection attacks.

chapters.title: Database Administration, Monitoring, and Tuning
chapters.description: Strategies for capacity planning, health monitoring, performance tuning, and high availability planning.

chapters.title: Cloud Databases and Database-as-a-Service (DBaaS)
chapters.description: Evaluate cloud deployment models, serverless databases, and strategies for migrating to the cloud.

chapters.title: Big Data Architectures and Real-time Processing
chapters.description: Explore data lakes, stream processing with Kafka, and the emergence of vector databases for AI workloads.

parts.title: Part I: Fundamentals of Data and Databases
Part III: Logical Database Design and Normalization
Part IV: Physical Database Design and Implementation
Part V: Transaction Management and Concurrency
Part VII: Database Security and Administration
Part VIII: Emerging Trends and Modern Applications
